chunks.text: On a back street of the city, tucked away, there is a used bookshop.
It is not a so - called tourist spot
but it is known to lovers of books.
The owner is a gentleman in his seventies who has been selling books from this place for more than thirty years.
When you step inside, the smell of old books surrounds you.
The shelves are filled with books up to the ceiling.
Literature, history, philosophy, science.
Whatever sort of book you want, you can find it if you search.
The owner is an expert on books.
When a customer says a title
within a few minutes he remembers where on the shelf it is.
He can tell you the plot of any book.
The shop has a silence like a unique chamber.
Customers speak in low voices and handle the books properly.
Sometimes a rare out - of - print book is brought in.
The owner promptly buys it and sells it at an appropriate price.
To deliver a book of worth to its next owner — that is his mission.
This is the age of e - books.
Sales of paper books are decreasing.
Used bookshops too are similarly shrinking in number.
But the owner is not pessimistic.
" A paper book has a charm that an e - book does not," he says.
" The feel in your hand, the sound of turning a page
the weight of the book itself.
Unlike books on a screen
you can line them on a shelf and make them part of your home.
In this shop, I have met many treasure - like books.
The prices are low, but the contents are superior.
The owner opens the shutter every morning, arranges the books, and waits for customers.
" I will continue as long as I can continue," he says.
May this quiet place remain, at least a little longer.
That is the wish of everyone who loves books.
